notes: Strong SOLID adherence throughout. The codebase defines 35 protocols with focused, role-specific names (the Swift '-ing' suffix convention: AppScanning, RunningAppChecking, ClaudeCodeAgentProcessExecuting, LaunchAtLoginInstalling, CodeSignatureVerifying, etc.) which is direct evidence of good ISP and DIP. No god classes — files with high method counts (ScheduledScanService.swift, MCPServerStatusStore.swift) contain MULTIPLE small types (protocol + default-impl extension + concrete impl + auxiliary types), not single oversized types. No type-switching OCP violations. No LSP violations (no fatalError('not implemented')). The Models layer is clean: only CleanupNarrative.swift imports SwiftUI, and only because it owns an EnvironmentKey/EnvironmentValues extension — which is the canonical SwiftUI pattern for that type, not a layering violation.

positive_signals: Protocol-oriented design with role-specific protocols (-ing suffix)
Default factory functions (defaultScheduledScanInstaller, defaultScheduledScanNotifier) for DIP
Sendable conformance on protocols enables compile-time concurrency safety
AnyObject + Sendable for class-bound protocols indicates intentional reference semantics
